B2C (Business to Consumer): Refers to individual retail customers.
B2B (Business to Business): Refers to corporate or enterprise customers.
WHOLESALE: Refers to telecom operators or partners purchasing services or capacity.

GAS (Gross Activations):the number of newly activated lines/subscriptions during a period. Activation logic may vary depending on context (billing start date, first recharge, first network event, CRM activation date).
FTTH (Fiber To The Home): Fixed high-speed internet service delivered through fiber optic infrastructure directly to the customer’s premises.
FTTH ACQUISITION: New FTTH installation for a customer who did not previously have an active fixed fiber service.
FTTH MIGRATION: Upgrade of an existing customer from a legacy fixed technology (such as ADSL or IDAR 4G/5G) to FTTH.
ADSL (Asymmetric Digital Subscriber Line): Fixed broadband internet service delivered through copper telephone lines.
Mobile: Mobile telecommunications service provided through cellular network infrastructure, including voice, SMS, and mobile data subscriptions.
Prepaid: Mobile service model where customers pay in advance before consuming services (voice, SMS, data). Usage is deducted from the available balance through recharges or top-ups.
Postpaid: Mobile service model where customers consume services first and are billed periodically (usually monthly) based on their subscription plan and usage.

IDAR: Portable fixed internet service provided through mobile network infrastructure. It uses 4G or 5G connectivity to deliver internet access via a wireless router. In some contexts, “IDAR” may refer collectively to both IDAR 4G and IDAR 5G offers.
IDAR 4G: Portable internet service delivered using 4G mobile network technology through a wireless router.
IDAR 5G: Portable internet service delivered using 5G mobile network technology through a wireless router, offering higher speeds and lower latency compared to IDAR 4G.
WIN: Commercial B2C postpaid mobile subscription offer.
FLEXI: Commercial B2C postpaid mobile subscription offer.

GOCOM, IWACO, SAHATEL, TENOR, AUTRE: Distribution partners (sales channels) responsible for selling and activating prepaid services. These entities represent external distributors through which customer activations may occur.
VI (Vente Indirecte): Prepaid activations performed through indirect distribution partners (distributors such as GOCOM, IWACO, SAHATEL, TENOR, etc.).
VS (Vente Street): Prepaid activations performed directly by street sales teams in the field.
Payment Channel: Method used by customers to pay (e.g., WMN, EBANKING, MTC, M2T, DISTRIBUTEUR, INWI DISTRIB, AUTRE).
WMN (WANA MONEY): Payment made via WANA Money (mobile wallet).
EBANKING: Payment made via banking applications or online banking services.
MTC: Payment made via web/online payment channel.
M2T: Payment made via ATM (GAB) channel.
DISTRIBUTEUR: Payment made through a retail partner (external distributor).
INWI DISTRIB: Payment made in an INWI agency/store.
AUTRE: Payment made through other unspecified channels.
MULTI: Commercial offer representing multi-line or bundled subscriptions (e.g., combining multiple services or lines under a single package).
Complaint Status: Indicates the current processing state of a customer complaint within the service management workflow.
Ouvert: Complaint has been registered and is currently under investigation or processing. No final resolution has been provided yet.
Standby: Complaint processing is temporarily paused, usually pending additional information, customer feedback, or external action.
Escaladé: Complaint has been escalated to a higher technical or managerial level due to complexity, priority, or unresolved issues.
Résolu: Complaint has been successfully handled and a resolution has been provided to the customer.
Annulé: Complaint has been cancelled, either due to customer withdrawal, duplication, or invalid request.
PROPRE: Refers to services delivered using INWI’s own infrastructure (own fiber network and internal technical resources).

VULA (Virtual Unbundled Local Access): Refers to FTTH services provided using infrastructure owned by a partner operator (IAM, Orange). INWI uses the partner’s fiber network to deliver the service to customers.

GAS B2B (KPI): Number of new B2B service activations during the selected period, determined by the billing start date, sourced from the bda_activations table, and segmented by technology (FTTH, ADSL, Mobile).

GAS CPE (KPI): Number of B2C prepaid customers considered activated after performing their first network event (call, SMS, or data session). The activation date corresponds to the date of this first recorded network activity. The KPI is sourced from the bda_activations table.

GAS CPR (KPI): Number of B2C prepaid customers considered activated after performing their first recharge. The activation date corresponds to the date of the first top-up transaction on the line. The KPI is sourced from the bda_activations table.

GAS CRM (KPI): Number of B2C prepaid customers considered activated based on the CRM activation date, which corresponds to the effective date of the line in Salesforce CRM. An Activation CRM refers to a customer activated in the CRM system who has not yet performed any network activity (call, SMS, or data). The KPI is sourced from the bda_activations table.

GAS HOME (KPI): Number of new fixed internet subscriptions activated during the selected period, determined by the billing start date and segmented by technology (ADSL, FTTH, IDAR 4G, IDAR 5G). The KPI is sourced from the bda_activations table.

GAS POSTPAID (KPI): Number of new B2C postpaid mobile activations during the selected period, determined by the billing start date. The KPI is segmented by subscription offer (e.g., WIN, FLEXI) and measures the volume of newly activated postpaid lines. The KPI is sourced from the bda_activations table.

GAS PREP PAR CANAL (KPI): Number of B2C prepaid activations during the selected period, determined according to the defined activation logic (CPE – first network event). The KPI is segmented by activation channel (GOCOM, IWACO, SAHATEL, TENOR, and others) and is sourced from the bda_activations table.

GAS PREP PAR TYPE (KPI): Number of B2C prepaid activations during the selected period, determined according to the CPE activation logic (first network event). The KPI is segmented by activation type (MRE, VI, VS) and is sourced from the bda_activations table.

ENCAISSEMENT (KPI): Total amount of payments collected during the selected period, excluding taxes. The KPI is segmented by customer segment (B2B, B2C Postpaid) or by commercial offer (FLEXI, WIN) and is sourced from the bda_encaissement table.

ENCAISSEMENT B2C PAR CANAL (KPI): Total amount collected from B2C customers during the selected period, excluding taxes. The KPI is segmented by payment channel (WMN, EBANKING, MTC, M2T, DISTRIBUTEUR, INWI DISTRIB, AUTRE) and is sourced from the bda_encaissement table.

ENCAISSEMENT B2C PAR OFFRE (KPI): Total amount collected from B2C customers during the selected period, excluding taxes. The KPI is segmented by commercial offer (ADSL, FTTH, IDAR, MOBILE, MULTI) and is sourced from the bda_encaissement table.

TAUX ENCAISSEMENT – B2B & WHOLESALE (KPI): Collection rate representing the percentage of invoiced amounts effectively collected during the selected period. It is calculated as: (Collected Amount / Invoiced Amount) × 100. The KPI applies to B2B and WHOLESALE segments and is sourced from the bda_encaissement table.

TAUX ENCAISSEMENT – B2C (KPI): Collection rate representing the percentage of invoiced amounts effectively collected from B2C customers during the selected period. It is calculated as: (Collected Amount / Invoiced Amount) × 100. The KPI is segmented by commercial offer (ADSL, FTTH, IDAR, MOBILE, MULTI) and is sourced from the bda_encaissement table.

DELAIS MOYEN RACCORDEMENTS PROPRE (KPI): Average time required to connect (install and activate) an FTTH customer line after the initial service request. The KPI measures the mean delay between the request date and the activation date and is sourced from the bda_ftth_delai_raccordement table.
DELAIS MOYEN RACCORDEMENTS VULA (KPI):

DELAIS MOYEN RACCORDEMENTS VULA (KPI): Average time required to connect an FTTH customer line when the service relies on a partner operator’s infrastructure (VULA). The KPI measures the mean delay between the service request date and the activation date for FTTH lines provisioned through external operators. It is sourced from the bda_ftth_delai_raccordement table.

DELAIS RACCORDEMENTS PROPRE – FTTH ACQUISITION (KPI): Number of FTTH connections completed on INWI’s own infrastructure during the selected period, segmented by delay range (≤ 1 day, 1–2 days, > 2 days). This KPI applies to FTTH ACQUISITION (new customer installations) and is sourced from the bda_ftth_delai_raccordement table.

DELAIS RACCORDEMENTS PROPRE – FTTH MIGRATION (KPI): Number of FTTH connections completed on INWI’s own infrastructure during the selected period, segmented by delay range (≤ 1 day, 1–2 days, > 2 days). This KPI applies to FTTH MIGRATION (customers migrating from ADSL or IDAR to FTTH) and is sourced from the bda_ftth_delai_raccordement table.

DELAIS RACCORDEMENTS VULA – FTTH ACQUISITION (KPI): Number of FTTH connections completed under the VULA offer during the selected period, segmented by delay range (≤ 1 day, 1–2 days, > 2 days). This KPI applies to FTTH ACQUISITION (new customer installations) and is sourced from the bda_ftth_delai_raccordement table.

DELAIS RACCORDEMENTS VULA – FTTH MIGRATION (KPI): Number of FTTH connections completed under the VULA offer during the selected period, segmented by delay range (≤ 1 day, 1–2 days, > 2 days). This KPI applies to FTTH MIGRATION (customers migrating from ADSL or IDAR to FTTH) and is sourced from the bda_ftth_delai_raccordement table.

DELAIS RESOLUTION FTTH (KPI): Number of FTTH complaints resolved during the selected period, segmented by resolution time range (≤ 4 hours, 4–24 hours, 24–48 hours, > 48 hours). The KPI is sourced from the bda_ftth_reclamations table.

RECLAMATIONS FTTH (KPI): Number of complaints registered for the FTTH offer during the selected period, segmented by complaint status (Annulé, Escaladé, Ouvert, Résolu, Standby). The KPI is sourced from the bda_ftth_reclamations table.
RECHARGE GLOBALE: Total recharge amount including tax recorded across the different INWI offers.
RECHARGE GLOBALE: Montant TTC de la recharge globale enregistré sur les differentes offres INWI

RECHARGE PAR CANAL: Montant TTC de recharge splitté par canal de recharge : Dealer pour la recharge rapide, scratch card pour les carte de grattage et electronique pour les differents cannaux digitaux, web, banking…

RECHARGE PAR CANAL: Recharge amount including tax split by recharge channel: Dealer for fast recharge, Scratch Card for prepaid voucher cards, and Electronic for digital channels such as web, banking, etc.
EOP MOBILE: KPI representing the total number of active mobile (SIM-based) customers at the end of the reporting period.
EOP HOME: KPI representing the total number of active Home customer subscriptions (i.e: iDar, ADSL...) at the end of the reporting period.
Dealer: The Dealer channel refers to physical retail points or authorized resellers where customers can recharge their mobile credit directly (terminal or USSD system)
Scratch Card: The Scratch Card channel refers to physical prepaid recharge cards
Electronic: The Electronic channel refers to digital recharge methods (i.e: Mobile banking apps, Website payments..).
RECHARGE PAR TRANCHE: Montant TTC de la recharge enregistré sur les differentes offres INWI et ventilé par tranche de recharge
RECHARGE PAR TRANCHE: Total recharge amount including tax recorded across the different INWI offers and broken down by recharge denomination.

RECHARGE PAR TYPE: Montant TTC de la recharge enregistré sur les differentes offres INWI et ventilé sur les plus importantes etoiles de la recharge : *1 pour la voix nationale, *2 pour le pay as you go, *3 pour la Data et *6 pour les reseaux sociaux illimités

RECHARGE PAR TYPE: Total recharge amount including tax recorded across the different WEI offers and allocated to the main recharge stars: *1 for national voice, *2 for Pay-As-You-Go, *3 for Data, and *6 for unlimited social media.
RECHARGEUR GLOBAL: Nombre de rechargeurs global enregistré sur les differentes offres INWI
RECHARGEUR GLOBAL: Total number of unique rechargers recorded across the various INWI offers.

RECHARGEUR PAR TYPE: Nombre de rechargeurs enregistré sur les differentes offres INWI et ventilé par etoile/type de la recharge : *1 pour la voix nationale, *2 pour le pay as you go, *3 pour la Data et *6 pour les reseaux sociaux illimités

RECHARGEUR PAR TYPE: Number of unique rechargers recorded across various INWI offers, broken down by top-up type (Star code): *1 for National Voice, *2 for Pay-As-You-Go, *3 for Data, and *6 for Unlimited Social Media.
REVENU BILLING: Le revenu facturé HT enregistrés sur les differentes offres B2B, OTEO et wholesale.
REVENU BILLING: Net Billed Revenue (excluding VAT) recorded across various B2B, OTEO, and Wholesale offers.
Otéo est une filiale de Wana Corporate qui offre des services télécoms aux entreprises installées à Casanearshore et Technopolis, deux zones offshore de excellence au Maroc
REVENU BILLING: Le revenu facturé HT enregistrés sur les differentes offres B2C postpayé : Mobile, ADSL, Fibre, FLEXI, WIN, IDAR et enfin IDAR 5G
REVENU BILLING: Net Billed Revenue (excluding VAT) recorded for various B2C Postpaid offers: Mobile, ADSL, Fiber, FLEXI, WIN, IDAR, and IDAR 5G
CHURN: Churn is the metric that tracks when a customer '"leaves" the operator.
In the postpaid segment, churn is recognized at the point of contract closure, encompassing both customer-led attrition and credit-related deactivations.

Prepaid churn is a behavioral metric governed by ANRT, which defines an active customer as one who generates revenue (call, SMS, data, or recharge) within a 90-day window. If a SIM card remains inactive for more than three months, it is legally classified as "churned". This periodic purging often leads to sharp YTD subscriber dips, representing the removal of "ghost" lines rather than a real-time loss of active users.
